exactly_identical.annotation:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR the court
TO make a section 8 order at any time during the course of the proceedings
EVEN THOUGH it is not in a position to dispose finally of those proceedings [source: section-11-3]
exactly_identical.groundtruth:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a court
TO make a section 8 order at any time during the course of the proceedings in question even though it is not in a position to dispose finally of those proceedings
WHEN/IF/WHERE has power to do so. [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11]

reasonably_equal.annotation:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR rules of court
TO specify periods within which specified steps must be taken in relation to proceedings in which such questions arise
reasonably_equal.groundtruth:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR Rules of court
TO — (a) specify periods within which specified steps must be taken in relation to proceedings in which such questions arise; and (b) make other provision with respect to such proceedings for the purpose of ensuring, so far as is reasonably practicable, that such questions are determined without delay.

same_meaning_substantial_paraphrase.annotation:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the court
TO give such directions as it considers appropriate for the purpose of ensuring, so far as is reasonably practicable, that the timetable is adhered to
WHEN any question of making a section 8 order, or any other question with respect to such an order, arises [source: section-11-1]
same_meaning_substantial_paraphrase.groundtruth:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a court
TO — (in the light of any provision in rules of court that is of the kind mentioned in subsection (2)(a) or (b)) (a) draw up a timetable with a view to determining the question without delay; and (b) give such directions as it considers appropriate for the purpose of ensuring, so far as is reasonably practicable, that that timetable is adhered to.
WHEN/IF/WHERE any question of making a section 8 order, or any other question with respect to such an order, arises in proceedings. [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11]

only_in_annotations.annotation:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the court
TO draw up a timetable with a view to determining the question without delay
WHEN any question of making a section 8 order, or any other question with respect to such an order, arises [source: section-11-1]

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR rules of court
TO make other provision with respect to such proceedings for the purpose of ensuring, so far as is reasonably practicable, that such questions are determined without delay

IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR a child arrangements order
TO have effect
IF the parents live together for a continuous period of more than six months [source: section-11-5]

IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR a child arrangements order
TO have effect
IF the parents live together for a continuous period of more than six months [source: section-11-6]

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a section 8 order
TO contain directions about how it is to be carried into effect

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a section 8 order
TO impose conditions which must be complied with by any person named in the order

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a section 8 order
TO be made to have effect for a specified period, or contain provisions which are to have effect for a specified period

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a section 8 order
TO make such incidental, supplemental or consequential provision as the court thinks fit

only_in_groundtruth.groundtruth:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a child arrangements order made with respect to a child
TO so far as it provides for the child to spend time or otherwise have contact with one of the child's parents at times when the child is living with the child's other parent, cease to have effect
WHEN/IF/WHERE the parents live together for a continuous period of more than six months. [source: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/11]

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a section 8 order
TO (a) contain directions about how it is to be carried into effect; (b) impose conditions which must be complied with by any person— (i) who is named in the order as a person with whom the child concerned is to live, spend time or otherwise have contact; (ii) who is a parent of the child ...; (iii) who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him; or (iv) with whom the child is living, and to whom the conditions are expressed to apply; (c) be made to have effect for a specified period, or contain provisions which are to have effect for a specified period; (d) make such incidental, supplemental or consequential provision as the court thinks fit.
